description: Phase 3.1 backfill manifest: clause-level regulatory tags added to existing cat-22 UCs, closing the remaining 34 tier-1 clause gaps for CMMC 2.0, ISO/IEC 27001:2013, NIST CSF 1.1 & 2.0, PCI-DSS v3.2.1, GDPR 2016/679, NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, and HIPAA Security Rule 2013-final. Each mapping was selected manually by semantic match: the target UC already detects or evidences the control named by the clause. No new UCs, no SPL changes.

mappings.assurance_rationale: CMMC AC.L2-3.1.1 Authorized access to systems — UC evidences CUI-classification drift on authenticated systems and is the CMMC Level 2 practice anchor for this control in the catalogue.
CMMC AC.L2-3.1.5 Least privilege — UC area 2 surfaces CUI-marked systems requiring privilege review, feeding the least-privilege practice for DoD contractors.
CMMC AU.L2-3.3.1 Create audit records — UC area 3 is the CMMC audit-record practice anchor, tracking creation of auditable events on CUI systems.
CMMC AU.L2-3.3.2 Ensure unique user traceability — UC area 4 evidences user-to-action traceability on CUI-classified systems.
CMMC AU.L2-3.3.5 Audit reporting and correlation — UC area 5 is the CMMC audit-correlation practice anchor, surfacing cross-source CUI-marked events for review.
CMMC CM.L2-3.4.1 Baseline configurations — UC area 6 evidences baseline integrity for CUI-scoped systems required by CMMC Level 2.
CMMC IR.L2-3.6.1 Incident handling capability — UC area 7 is the CMMC incident-response practice anchor, linking CUI-related events into an IR workflow.
CMMC SC.L2-3.13.8 Cryptographic mechanisms for CUI in transit — UC area 8 evidences encryption-posture telemetry for CUI flows between contractor systems.
CMMC SI.L2-3.14.6 Monitor for attacks — UC area 9 is the CMMC attack-monitoring practice anchor, surfacing security-relevant events on CUI systems.
ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.9.2.5 Review of user access rights — UC directly authors the recertification and review evidence for user access rights, matching the 2013 control verbatim.
ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.12.4.1 Event logging — UC evidences the event-log review programme required by the 2013 control, covering both completeness and review cadence.

ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.12.4.2 Protection of log information — UC detects forwarder-stopped/crash-loop conditions that would allow log gaps or tampering, a direct protection-of-log-information signal.
ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.12.4.3 Administrator and operator logs — UC is the administrator/operator activity log (sudo and RunAs) detection required by the 2013 control.

ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.16.1.2 Reporting information security events — UC evidences SOC monitoring cadence (queue depth vs shift roster) which is the operational control ensuring events are received, triaged and reported per the 2013 control.
NIST CSF v1.1 ID.AM-1 Physical devices and systems are inventoried — UC is the asset-inventory coverage detection, directly matching the subcategory.
NIST CSF v1.1 PR.AC-1 Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited — UC evidences identity authentication hardening and MFA coverage across the identity lifecycle.
NIST CSF v1.1 DE.AE-3 Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources and sensors — UC is the Detect Function coverage-gap analysis, quantifying multi-source event correlation.

NIST CSF 2.0 PR.AA-05 Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined, managed, enforced, and reviewed — UC evidences identity authentication hardening and MFA coverage, which is the access-permission enforcement signal.

NIST CSF 2.0 PR.PS-04 Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring — UC detects administrative-API logging-volume drops, a direct signal that log generation has regressed against baseline.

NIST CSF 2.0 DE.CM-09 Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events — UC covers continuous vulnerability exposure drift on critical servers, a concrete environment-monitoring signal.

NIST CSF 2.0 RS.AN-03 Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause — UC tracks IR playbook execution stage timestamps, directly evidencing incident analysis activity.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 3.4 PAN rendering unreadable — UC directly validates PAN masking in POS and web-receipt logs; maps 1:1 to v3.2.1 control and forward-compatible with v4.0 Requirement 3.5.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 8.2.3 Strong password parameters — UC evidences domain password-policy compliance via RSoP events, directly matching the v3.2.1 requirement.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.1 Audit trail linking access to user — UC builds the comprehensive audit trail for CDE administrator logons, linking every access to a specific user identity.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.2 Audit events required to be logged — UC detects missing Windows Security events per CDE host, directly enforcing the required-audit-events list.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.5 Log integrity — UC detects Splunk _audit tamper indicators (saved-search deletes, role changes) that would compromise log integrity, the direct v3.2.1 10.5 evidence.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.6 Log review — UC is the daily log-review workflow SLA tracker, producing the review-evidence artefact required by the v3.2.1 control.
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 11.4 Intrusion detection — UC baselines IDS/IPS alert volume and surfaces spikes, directly evidencing the v3.2.1 intrusion-detection control.
GDPR Art.16 Right to rectification — UC fulfilment tracker covers Art.15-22 data-subject rights end-to-end, including rectification workflow timestamps and SLA adherence.
GDPR Art.18 Right to restrict processing — UC enforces consent-withdrawal processing, the technical signal behind a restriction decision propagating across processing systems.
GDPR Art.21 Right to object — UC audits the consent mechanism against lawful-basis alignment, covering the opt-out / objection propagation that Art.21 requires.
NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 PS-4 Personnel Termination — UC directly tracks access-revocation within policy window on termination, matching the control's explicit workflow requirement.
NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 PT-3 Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes — UC enforces purpose-limitation across systems, providing the evidence that processing stays within declared purposes.
HIPAA Security Rule §164.308(a)(4) Information access management — UC detects role-based access violations (e.g. coders accessing medication admin), the direct information-access-management evidence.
